summary: Matched Link's up-special implementation by moving the ftLk_SpecialHi / ftLk_SpecialAirHi entry, anim, IASA, phys, collision, and helper routines out of ftLk_Init.s into a new C file, wiring that object into obj_files.mk, and updating headers/callers to use named helper APIs. The PR also renamed several fighter/common attribute fields and Link fighter-var/dat-attr offsets to more useful names, with broad callsite cleanup across other fighters that used aerial drift attributes. There were no review or issue comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftLink/ftLk_SpecialHi.c with C implementations for Link up-special routines: ftLk_SpecialHi_Enter, ftLk_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftLk_SpecialHi_Anim, ftLk_SpecialAirHi_Anim, IASA stubs, phys, collision, and helper functions.
Removed the matched ftLk_SpecialHi assembly range from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftLk_Init.s, leaving later ftLk_Init assembly intact.
Added ftLk_SpecialHi.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in the text object build.

Renamed item-facing Link helper symbols used from asm/melee/it/it_27CF.s: ftLk_800EBF20 became ftLk_SpecialHi_GetFv4, ftLk_800EBEEC became ftLk_SpecialHi_GetPosWithAdjustedY, and ftLk_800EBEC4 became ftLk_SpecialHi_ProcessPartLThumbNb.
Updated ftLk_SpecialHi.h to declare the new helper APIs and include Vec3 forward declarations via dolphin/mtx/forward.h.
Changed Link's remaining local assembly callback label .L_800EBF2C into global lbl_800EBF2C because ftLk_Init.s still stores that callback after nearby functions were moved to C.

Expanded ftLink forward/types headers with ftLink_FighterVars, ftLk_DatAttrs, and ftLk_MotionVars declarations, plus named dat-attr fields such as specialhi_pos_y_offset, specialairhi_drift_stick_mul, specialairhi_drift_max_mul, and specialhi_grav_mul.
Rebased ftLink_FighterVars field names from absolute-looking offsets such as x222C/x2234/x2244 to relative names x0/x8/x18 while preserving offset comments like fp+222C and fp+2244.

Renamed common fighter aerial drift attributes in ft/types.h from x174_AerialDriftStickMult and x17C_AerialDriftMax to air_drift_stick_mul and air_drift_max, with callsite updates in ftcommon and several character special-move files.
Adjusted Link and CLink init/appeal/inline code to use the renamed fighter-var fields, especially replacing x2244 with x18 for the held/created item GObj pointer.
Removed ftLk_Init_804D92F8 and ftLk_Init_804D92FC float constants from the remaining asm sdata2 block after the moved C code used literal 0 and 1 parameters instead.
smart_moves: Kept the decomp focused on a coherent move module: the new file contains the complete ground/air up-special lifecycle plus the small helpers that item code needs.
Used semantic helper names for item-facing functions instead of preserving anonymous ftLk_800EBE* names, making cross-system calls in it_27CF.s searchable and self-documenting.

Preserved an assembly callback still referenced by un-decompiled Link code by promoting .L_800EBF2C to lbl_800EBF2C, avoiding accidental loss of a relocatable symbol when splitting code across objects.
Encoded the landing/ground-to-air transition motion flags as a C MotionFlags constant rather than leaving only the raw 0x0C4C508A assembly immediate.
Centralized repeated aerial physics logic by making ftLk_SpecialHi_Phys call ftLk_SpecialAirHi_Phys when Link is airborne, matching the assembly branch while reducing duplicated source logic.
Named broad common attributes air_drift_stick_mul and air_drift_max while updating all known callsites in the same PR, avoiding a mixed old/new naming state.
Documented ftLk_SpecialHi_GetPosWithAdjustedY in the header with its out-parameter behavior and the dat-attr field that drives the Y adjustment.
decomp_lessons: When moving a block out of a larger asm file, audit nearby labels that are still referenced by the remaining assembly; local .L labels may need exported lbl_ names if address-taking remains.
Small helper functions used by item assembly are good opportunities for behavior-based names, because they clarify both the fighter module and item callsites.
Dat-attribute field naming can unblock clean C for move physics: Link's up-special needed named gravity, drift, velocity, and Y-offset fields rather than raw x28/x34/x38/x3C/x40/x44 offsets.
Common attribute renames should be propagated broadly in the same matching PR when they affect many fighters; otherwise future decomp work inherits inconsistent struct vocabulary.
Relative fighter-var names with explicit offset comments are safer than embedding absolute fp offsets in field names, especially inside per-character substructs.
MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays were used to account for stack layout in several functions; keep them isolated behind MUST_MATCH and mark them as temporary matching scaffolding.
For simple no-op callbacks, empty C functions can replace bare blr assembly for IASA routines.
naming_conventions: ftLk_SpecialHi_* and ftLk_SpecialAirHi_* are used for Link ground/air up-special lifecycle callbacks: Enter, Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
Item-facing Link helpers were renamed with action-oriented names: ftLk_SpecialHi_ProcessPartLThumbNb, ftLk_SpecialHi_GetPosWithAdjustedY, and ftLk_SpecialHi_GetFv4.
Common fighter drift fields now use semantic snake_case names: air_drift_stick_mul and air_drift_max.
Link dat-attrs gained move-specific names with the specialhi/specialairhi prefix: specialhi_pos_y_offset, specialairhi_drift_stick_mul, specialairhi_drift_max_mul, and specialhi_grav_mul.
Per-character fighter vars in ftLink_FighterVars were changed from absolute-looking names x222C/x2230/x2234/x2244 to relative x0/x4/x8/x18 names while keeping fp+offset comments.
Remaining exported assembly labels use lbl_ADDRESS form when they must be globally referenced from assembly.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the matched assembly body for addresses 0x800EBA4C through 0x800EBF20 from ftLk_Init.s and replaced it with C in ftLk_SpecialHi.c.
Added the new C object before the remaining ftLk_Init.s object in obj_files.mk, matching the expected text order for the split.
Converted .L_800EBF2C to global lbl_800EBF2C so ftLk_SpecialS_Enter and ftLk_SpecialAirS_Enter in the remaining asm can still store the callback pointer.
Used MUST_MATCH guarded dummy stack arrays in several functions to reproduce unused stack allocation patterns seen in the original assembly.
Replaced raw Fighter_ChangeMotionState constants with named motion IDs ftLk_MS_SpecialHi and ftLk_MS_SpecialAirHi plus Ft_MF_None or a composed MotionFlags constant.

Matched accessory/effect behavior by setting x21BC_callback_Accessory4 to a static onAccessory4 function, spawning effect IDs 1211/1212, setting x2219_b0, and installing efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.
Preserved physics calls from assembly with ftCommon_8007D494 and ftCommon_8007D344 using common attrs grav, terminal_vel, air_drift_stick_mul, and air_drift_max scaled by Link dat attrs.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or submitted reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename remaining ftLk_DatAttrs fields x30/x34/x40 or clarify their semantics.
Find PRs that decompile the remaining ftLk_Init.s functions around lbl_800EBF2C and ftLk_SpecialS.
Find uses of ftLk_SpecialHi_GetPosWithAdjustedY and ftLk_SpecialHi_GetFv4 in item code to confirm final helper naming.
Search for follow-up cleanup removing MUST_MATCH unused stack arrays in ftLk_SpecialHi.c.
Search for additional ftCo_DatAttrs renames around x178_AerialDriftBase and x180_AerialFriction.
